page_title: Super visa for parents and grandparents – How long you can stay in Canada - Canada.ca
h1: How long you can stay in Canada

h2: If you applied on or after June 22, 2023
h3: You entered Canada on or after June 22, 2023

sections.heading: If you applied on or after June 22, 2023
items: You’re eligible to stay for 5 years at a time.

sections.heading: If you applied before June 22, 2023
items: The date you enter Canada with a valid super visa may also affect how long you can stay.

sections.heading: You entered Canada before June 22, 2023
items: You can only stay in Canada for the length of time the border officer granted you when you entered Canada.
For example, if you were granted 2 years, you can stay in Canada for 2 years.
However, if you want a longer stay, you have 2 options. You can either
The border officer may let you stay for 5 years if you still meet all the eligibility requirements for a super visa.
bullets: leave Canada before the end of your authorized stay and re-enter Canada, or
apply to extend your stay in Canada and be approved for an extension of up to 2 years

sections.heading: You entered Canada on or after June 22, 2023
items: You’re eligible to stay for 5 years at a time. If a border officer grants you entry to Canada, you can stay for 5 years, even if the border officer doesn’t stamp your passport.

The border officer may let you stay for 5 years if you still meet all the eligibility requirements for a super visa.
